requirements.summary: Import useSport hook and destructure sportStatus, currentSport, and deactivateSport in ActivateSportScreen, then derive isActive boolean from sportStatus
acceptanceCriteria: ActivateSportScreen imports useSport from SportContext without errors
sportStatus, currentSport, and deactivateSport are correctly destructured from useSport()
isActive evaluates to true when sportStatus is 'active' and false otherwise

file_operations.description: Add import statement for useSport from '../context/SportContext' at the top of the file. Inside the component function, add the destructuring assignment: const { sportStatus, currentSport, deactivateSport } = useSport(). Then derive the isActive boolean: const isActive = sportStatus === 'active'. Ensure these additions integrate with the existing component logic without breaking the current handleActivate flow.

requirements.summary: Replace static activation button label with dynamic label that displays 'Deactivate {currentSport}' when active or 'Activate My Sport' when inactive
acceptanceCriteria: Button label reads 'Activate My Sport' when no sport is active
Button label reads 'Deactivate {Sport}' (e.g. 'Deactivate Soccer') when a sport is active
Label updates reactively when sport status changes without a page reload

file_operations.description: Locate the activation button element in the JSX. Replace its static text/children with a dynamic expression: {isActive ? `Deactivate ${currentSport}` : 'Activate My Sport'}. This will cause the button label to reactively update based on the isActive derived state and the currentSport value from SportContext.

requirements.summary: Update button onClick handler to call deactivateSport when active or handleActivate when inactive, ensuring sport filtering behavior persists correctly
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the button when sport is active calls deactivateSport() and transitions sportStatus away from 'active'
Tapping the button when sport is inactive calls the existing handleActivate() function
No runtime crashes occur during either action
Map and Events pages continue to auto-filter by the active sport after activation
After deactivation, Map and Events pages revert to unfiltered or default state

file_operations.description: Modify the button's onClick prop to conditionally execute either deactivateSport() or handleActivate() based on isActive. Implementation: onClick={() => { if (isActive) { deactivateSport(); } else { handleActivate(); } }}. Preserve the existing handleActivate function intact. Verify that this change allows the button to toggle between activation and deactivation states without breaking navigation or state management logic.
